macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Adds 178,000 Jobs in March, Beating Forecasts Despite Iran War Concerns
items.summary: The US economy added 178,000 nonfarm payroll jobs in March, surpassing expectations of 60,000, with unemployment falling to 4.3%; however, markets remain cautious due to the ongoing Iran war impacting growth and inflation.[1][2]

items.title: Markets May Reject Strong Jobs Data Amid Iran War Recession Fears
items.summary: Despite upbeat March jobs report with payrolls topping forecasts and unemployment at 4.3%, stocks and bonds may decline as the Fed struggles with inflation from the Iran war, while labor force participation hits a low.[2]

items.title: Iran Air Strikes Cause Oil Surge to Near $100, Trigger Four-Week Stock Slump
items.summary: Air strikes on Iran disrupted energy supplies, driving WTI oil prices toward $100 and leading to a four-week decline in stocks amid March market turmoil.[3]

items.title: Atlanta Fed Downgrades Q1 Growth to 1% Amid Flat Job Market and Rising Inflation
items.summary: The Atlanta Fed cut its first-quarter growth estimate to nearly 1%, citing a flat job market, inflation above target, and risks from the Iran war.[1]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: $156 Million in Crypto Futures Liquidations Over Past 24 Hours
items.summary: Crypto futures markets saw $156 million in liquidations, led by ETH at $47.06 million and BTC at $46.07 million, followed by SOL and XRP. This underscores ongoing market volatility amid stable prices.

items.title: Bitcoin Stabilizes Near $67,000 as Markets Stay Muted
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $67,000 with minimal movement after recent volatility between $65,700 and $69,200, while the total crypto market cap held below $2.4 trillion. Ethereum hovered near $2,050, down slightly.

items.title: Pi Network's PI Token Stabilizes Above $0.17 After Losses
items.summary: PI token steadied above $0.17 following a sharp decline, pausing its downward trend amid broader flat altcoin performance. VeChain gained 9% while HASH dropped 10%.

items.title: SIREN Token Surges 45.8% to $0.256 in 24 Hours
items.summary: SIREN token experienced a dramatic 45.76% price increase, reaching $0.256 and entering the top 170 by market cap. The surge marks one of the strongest altcoin moves in the period.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Microsoft Launches Three New In-House AI Models Targeting OpenAI and Google
items.summary: Microsoft released three foundational AI models built entirely in-house, including a state-of-the-art speech transcription system, as a direct challenge to competitors.

items.title: Arcee Releases Open-Source Trinity-Large-Thinking Model for Enterprise Customization
items.summary: Arcee unveiled Trinity-Large-Thinking, a powerful U.S.-made open-source AI model that enterprises can download and customize.

items.title: OpenAI Expands COO Brad Lightcap’s Role as AGI Chief Takes Medical Leave
items.summary: OpenAI is reshuffling leadership with COO Brad Lightcap taking on expanded responsibilities amid the AGI chief's medical leave and looming IPO plans.

items.title: Take-Two Lays Off AI Team Head and Members Following CEO's Doubts
items.summary: Take-Two Interactive laid off the head of its AI team and several members after the CEO expressed doubts about AI.

items.title: Claude Subscribers Lose Access to Third-Party Tools Unless Paying More
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude subscribers have lost access to OpenClaw and other third-party tools unless they upgrade to higher payment tiers.

items.title: Gemini Overtakes Perplexity as No. 2 Chatbot for Website Referrals
items.summary: Google's Gemini has surpassed Perplexity to become the second-leading chatbot in driving website referrals.

items.title: Shopify Enables Merchants to Sell Directly via ChatGPT and AI Channels
items.summary: Shopify has integrated features allowing merchants to sell products directly through ChatGPT and other AI channels.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: State of Decay 3 Alpha Playtest Announced
items.summary: Microsoft has announced an alpha playtest for State of Decay 3, allowing selected players to sign up via the official website for early access. The announcement highlights improved four-player co-op compared to State of Decay 2, though no release date was revealed.

items.title: Hytale Reveals Major Updates Including Infinite World Height
items.summary: Hytale announced significant changes such as infinite world height and the potential elimination of workstations, alongside a massive overhaul of crafting, progression, exploration, and end-game content in true early access style.

items.title: Fortnite Teases New Content Including Save the World F2P and Toy Story Collaboration
items.summary: Fortnite revealed upcoming updates, including Save the World going free-to-play on the same day as a major event, with Toy Story content arriving very soon.

items.title: Last Epoch Gears Up for Uber Abberoth Content
items.summary: Last Epoch is preparing for the Uber Abberoth update, as covered in recent content focusing on builds, guides, and season developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Sony Interactive Entertainment Acquires Cinemersive Labs for 2D-to-3D Conversion Tools
items.summary: Sony has acquired UK-based startup Cinemersive Labs, which develops tools to convert 2D photos and videos into 3D volumetric photographs. The startup will join Sony's Visual Computing Group to enhance PlayStation-related technologies.

items.title: OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap Transitions to Special Projects Role; Fidji Simo on Medical Leave
items.summary: OpenAI's COO Brad Lightcap is moving to a special projects role reporting to Sam Altman, while another top executive Fidji Simo takes medical leave for several weeks. This leadership change occurs amid ongoing company developments.
